rationale: Fingerprint `ARR` — this 44-gen bucket is dominated by `tsh` with `t => [JSON.parse(t).active.username]` (42 gens) which returns `["string"]`, i.e. an array of raw strings. The oracle's `compareResults` rejects non-object items (`js[i] is not an object`), so those 42 will remain `fail`/`oracle_error` regardless of input. Only the 2 `ng` generators have a try/catch returning `[]` on any parse failure. Feeding `"[]"`, `""`, garbage routes `ng` through its try/catch (or through `Object.entries([])` = `[]`), yielding `[]`. Rust `split_lines+filter_empty+json_extract(sentinel)` on these inputs also yields `[]` (no object has the sentinel key). Expected: 2 `ng` generators pass, 42 `tsh` generators get `js_exception` / `fail` — shape-too-loose.
